L2().details: Sets up the L2 contracts. Depends on `L1()` being called first.

_setupSafe().details: Sets up a Safe with default parameters. The SafeInstance will have 3 owners and a threshold of 2.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256).details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

_setupSafe(uint256[],uint256,uint256,(bo.details: Sets up a Safe with the given parameters.

params.advancedParams: The advanced parameters for the Safe initialization.
params.initialBalance: The initial balance of the Safe.
params.ownerPKs: The public keys of the owners.

disableUpgradedFork().details: Disables upgrade mode for testing. By default the fork testing env will be upgraded to the latest      implementation. This can be used to disable the upgrade which, is useful for tests targeting the upgrade      process itself.

enableAltDA().details: Enables alternative data availability mode for testing

getSafe(address).details: Returns the Safe instance with the given address.

params._safe: The address of the Safe instance to return.

returns._0: The Safe instance with the given address.

returnIfForkTest(string).details: Returns early when running against a forked production network. Useful for allowing a portion of a test      to run.

skipIfCoverage().details: Skips tests when running in coverage mode.

skipIfForkTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network.

skipIfOpsRepoTest(string).details: Skips tests when running against a forked production network using the superchain ops repo.

artifacts().notice: The address of the Artifacts contract. Set into state by Deployer.setUp() with `etch` to avoid         mutating any nonces. MUST not have constructor logic.

isForkTest().notice: Indicates whether a test is running against a forked production network.

testFuzz_pause_differentNoncesAlreadyPau.notice: Tests that pause() succeeds when called with two different nonces after the         superchain has already been paused between calls.

testFuzz_pause_differentNonces_succeeds(.notice: Tests that pause() succeeds when called with two different nonces if the         SuperchainConfig contract is not paused between calls.

testFuzz_pause_validParameters_succeeds(.notice: Tests that pause() successfully pauses when called by the deputy.

test_pause_withinMillionGas_succeeds().notice: Tests that pause() succeeds within 1 million gas.
